prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh sweetheart, well, from what I understand, the CPU is sort of like the brain of the computer. It's this little square piece tucked away inside the machine, and your grandpa once told me it does all the thinking and figuring — you know, adding up numbers and telling the other parts what to do. I think "CPU" stands for something like "Central Processing Unit," though don't quote me on that, dear. When you click something or ask the computer to do a task, that little brain is the one actually working it all out, very quickly I'm told. It's really quite marvelous when you think about it, though I couldn't begin to tell you how they fit so much cleverness into something so small. Now, why don't you go ask your father to explain the rest — he's much better with these things than I am.
